```text
ARCHITECT DECISIONS:
- Wire-smoke runner now declares runtime-owner-canaries and runtime-owner-scaffold capabilities: the packet graph (post-canary-oracle, post-scaffold-oracle) already declares these capabilities against wire-smoke, but runners.toml only listed wire-compatibility, so the capability evidence join would silently produce nothing. Closes a real manifest-vs-packet drift left in the prior attempt.
- Object vocabulary is the single source of truth for the scaffold type surface: work-packets.jsonl `runtime-owner-2-scaffold-types` was re-enumerating the type list, violating the "single source of truth" rule in CLAUDE.md. Note now points at object-vocabulary.tsv. Adding a scaffold type requires the architect doc to be updated first.
- `SlotId` registered as a newtype, not a bare u32 alias: the dispatch signature `RuntimeOwner::dispatch(slot_id, parsed)` and `RuntimeEvent::WakeBlocked { slot_id, .. }` referenced a typed slot id that was never declared. Forcing a newtype prevents accidental crossing with db_index / replica_id at the type level.
- Completion non-goals now mirror the locked architect decisions: TLS owner-loop migration, adding a poller workspace dep, public speed-parity from a non-default mode, and authoring a soak runner before its script exists — all reified in `harness/completion.toml [project].non_goals` so a future planning packet can't silently relax them.
- Planned `runtime-owner-soak` runner reified as a `[[planned_runner]]` placeholder (not a real `[[runner]]`): names the missing `harness/bench/run-soak.sh` script and points at the TODO(human) gate. Prevents a future translator from inventing a runner row with a non-existent script.
- Lifecycle-map gains a top-level `[invariants]` table: encodes the five cross-artifact rules in machine-readable form so the loop, not just prose, enforces them.

FILES UPDATED:
- harness/runners.toml: wire-smoke capability list expanded; new `[[planned_runner]]` row for runtime-owner-soak with status=blocked-on-human, script_required, expected_capabilities.
- harness/completion.toml: `[project].non_goals` extended with TLS/poller/public-claim/soak-author locks; added `binding_architecture_artifacts` and `decision_evidence_root` fields pointing at the binding artifacts and the hotspot evidence run.
- harness/architecture/object-vocabulary.tsv: added `SlotId` newtype row.
- harness/work-packets.jsonl: `runtime-owner-2-scaffold-types` note no longer re-enumerates allowed types; now defers to object-vocabulary.tsv as authoritative, and locks SlotId as a newtype.
- harness/architecture/lifecycle-map.toml: added top-level `[invariants]` table encoding five cross-artifact rules.
- harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md: added "Cross-Artifact Synchronization (locked 2026-05-22)" section that records the same five rules in prose, with a single-architect escalation requirement to change them.
- docs/RUNTIME_OWNERSHIP_PLAN.md: appended "Refinement 2026-05-22 (re-attempt)" pointing at the just-landed consistency edits and explicitly stating they do not change the production direction, packet graph, or TODO(human) gates.

PACKETS CREATED OR CHANGED:
- runtime-owner-2-scaffold-types (translator): note rewritten to defer scaffold type enumeration to object-vocabulary.tsv and add an explicit "SlotId must be a newtype" requirement. Same dependencies, same targets, same forbidden list.
- No new packets added. Existing chain (baseline-{oracle,profile-matrix,hotspots} -> 0-faithful-map -> 1-canary-corpus -> post-canary-oracle -> 2-scaffold-types -> post-scaffold-{oracle,profile-matrix,hotspots}) is unchanged and remains the entire safe-dispatch surface until the TODO(human) gates are answered.

HUMAN QUESTIONS:
- Poller dependency for runtime-owner-3 (real owner loop): mio (recommended) vs polling vs tokio vs raw libc. Approval required to add to crates/redis-server/Cargo.toml.
- TLS migration sequencing: never in this port, follow-up after owner default, or pre-default? Gates whether crates/redis-core/src/connection.rs may change in owner-loop packets.
- I/O thread parity with upstream's trySendPollJobToIOThreads: skip, follow-up packet, or match upstream now? Default recommendation: follow-up.
- Soak runner scope: harness/bench/run-soak.sh does not exist; before any public performance claim, an explicit runtime-owner-soak runner must land. Need: workloads, duration, allowed regression band.

NEXT SAFE DISPATCH:
- runtime-owner-1-canary-corpus (translator): unblocked. Its note and the now-aligned wire-smoke runner manifest let post-canary-oracle produce real capability evidence under both `wire-compatibility` and `runtime-owner-canaries`.
